steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what the text most strongly suggests about southern sea otters in environments where snails, mussels, and clams are present. The passage establishes that snails are energy-poor compared to mussels and clams, and that tool-using otters can access mussels and clams while non-tool-users are limited to snails.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — non-tool-using otters will likely exhibit less tooth damage than tool-using otters: The passage says the opposite — snail-eaters (non-tool-users) exhibit LESS tooth damage than tool-users. But this is not the strongest suggestion about the energy-environment scenario the question targets. ✗
  • Choice B — non-tool-using otters will have more robust health than tool-using otters: The passage states that tool-using otters actually gain fitness benefits from higher-quality prey (mussels and clams), so this is contradicted by the text. ✗
  • Choice C — non-tool-using otters must consume larger amounts of prey to meet their energy requirements: Because snails are energy-poor and mussels/clams are energy-rich, otters restricted to snails must eat greater quantities to obtain the same energy that tool-users get from fewer mussels and clams. This follows directly from the passage. ✓
  • Choice D — non-tool-using otters spend less time foraging than tool-using otters: The passage indicates the opposite — otters limited to energy-poor snails would need to forage more, not less, to meet their energy needs. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
